other_observers: Chris Wood; none of which I am aware
duration: five to ten seconds in two views
description: An obvious brown thrush, it was larger than a Swainson's Thrush in the area and much redder. Though I saw it only briefly, the features that I did see were enough to convince me of the ID.  The crown, nape, and back were bright orangish. The dark eye was surrounded by a bold white eyering. The auriculars were streaked black and white. The underparts were white with large, bold black spots.Behaviors: Skulking in dense cover in the drainage north of the pond.Call: No vocalizations heardPlumage: Basic
